title: Ozothamnus kempei
taxonomy: Ozothamnus kempei is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification places Ozothamnus kempei within the order Asterales and the clade Angiosperms. As a member of the genus Ozothamnus, Ozothamnus kempei shares characteristics with other shrub-like plants native to Australia. The plant is further categorized into the tribe Gnaphalieae, which comprises a diverse group of daisy-like plants. Within this tribe, Ozothamnus kempei is one of several species native to Western Australia.
uses: Ozothamnus kempei has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and while its specific medicinal uses are not well-documented, it is sometimes used to make a tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Ozothamnus kempei, also known as Lemon Ironwood or Silvertop, is a small shrub native to Australia, characterized by its silvery foliage and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozothamnus kempei include terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ozothamnus kempei is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds.
